title: Persecution is from the Devil.

source.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Seeing therefore that such are the commands of our Saviour, and that such is the conduct of the Saints, let these persons, to whom one cannot give a name suitable to their character,—let them, I say, tell us, from whom they learnt to persecute?

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: They cannot say, from the Saints.

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: No, but from the Devil (that is the only answer which is left to them);—from him who says, ‘I will persue, I will overtake.’

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Our Lord commanded to flee, and the saints fled: but persecution is a device of the Devil, and one which he desires to exercise against all.

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Let them say then, to which we ought to submit ourselves; to the words of the Lord, or to their fabrications?

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Whose conduct ought we to imitate, that of the Saints, or that of those whose example these men have adopted?

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: But since it is likely they cannot determine this question (for, as Esaias said, their minds and their consciences are blinded, and they think ‘bitter to be sweet,’ and ‘light darkness’) let some one come forth from among us Christians, and put them to rebuke, and cry with a loud voice, ‘It is better to trust in the Lord, than to attend to the foolish sayings of these men; for the “words” of the Lord have “eternal life,” but the things which these utter are full of iniquity and blood.’
